attractions.name: Colosseum
attractions.description: The iconic Flavian Amphitheatre, the largest ancient amphitheatre ever built.
attractions.address: Piazza del Colosseo, 1, 00184 Roma RM, Italy

attractions.name: Roman Forum
attractions.description: The sprawling ruins of the heart of the ancient Roman Empire.
attractions.address: Largo della Salara Vecchia, 5/6, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

attractions.name: St. Peter's Basilica
attractions.description: A masterpiece of Renaissance architecture and the center of the Catholic world.
attractions.address: Piazza San Pietro, 00120 Città del Vaticano

attractions.name: Pantheon
attractions.description: A former Roman temple, now a church, famous for its massive unreinforced concrete dome.
attractions.address: Piazza della Rotonda, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

attractions.name: Trevi Fountain
attractions.description: The largest Baroque fountain in the city and one of the most famous in the world.
attractions.address: Piazza di Trevi, 00187 Roma RM, Italy

attractions.name: Castel Sant'Angelo
attractions.description: A towering cylindrical fortress originally built as a mausoleum for Emperor Hadrian.

attractions.name: Piazza Navona
attractions.description: A grand square built on the site of the Stadium of Domitian, featuring Bernini's Fountain of the Four Rivers.

attractions.name: Spanish Steps
attractions.description: A monumental stairway of 135 steps climbing a steep slope between the Piazza di Spagna and Piazza Trinità dei Monti.
attractions.address: Piazza di Spagna, 00187 Roma RM, Italy

restaurants.name: Roscioli Salumeria con Cucina
restaurants.description: A legendary deli and restaurant known for its carbonara and extensive wine list.
restaurants.address: Via dei Giubbonari, 21, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

restaurants.name: Da Enzo al 29
restaurants.description: A tiny, authentic trattoria in Trastevere famous for classic Roman dishes.
restaurants.address: Via dei Vascellari, 29, 00153 Roma RM, Italy

restaurants.name: Armando al Pantheon
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant serving traditional Roman cuisine right next to the Pantheon.
restaurants.address: Salita de' Crescenzi, 31, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

restaurants.name: Pizzeria da Baffetto
restaurants.description: Famous for thin-crust Roman-style pizza in a bustling, no-frills setting.
restaurants.address: Via del Governo Vecchio, 114, 00186 Roma RM, Italy

hidden_gems.name: Capuchin Crypt
hidden_gems.description: A macabre yet fascinating series of small chapels decorated with the bones of 4,000 friars.
hidden_gems.address: Via Vittorio Veneto, 27, 00187 Roma RM, Italy

nightlife.name: Trastevere District
nightlife.description: The heart of Rome's nightlife, filled with narrow cobblestone streets, bars, and pubs.

shopping.name: Via del Corso
shopping.description: The main shopping street in Rome, featuring high-street brands and department stores.

local_tips: Always carry a reusable water bottle; Rome is full of 'nasoni' (drinking fountains) with free, cold, high-quality water.
Validate your bus or tram ticket immediately upon boarding to avoid hefty fines.
Avoid eating at restaurants with 'tourist menus' or pictures of food outside; stick to small, crowded trattorias.
August is the month when many locals go on vacation; expect some smaller family-run shops to be closed.

safety.general: Rome is generally safe, but be extremely vigilant against pickpockets in crowded tourist areas like the Colosseum, Termini station, and on public transport.
safety.water_safety: Tap water is safe and delicious throughout the city.
safety.transport_safety: Be cautious of unlicensed taxis; always use official white taxis with a meter.

culture.overview: Rome is a blend of ancient history and modern 'La Dolce Vita'.
culture.etiquette: Dress modestly when entering churches (shoulders and knees covered). Tipping is not mandatory but rounding up the bill is appreciated.

transport.getting_around: The historic center is best explored on foot. For longer distances, use the Metro (Lines A and B) or the extensive bus network.
transport.airport: Leonardo da Vinci-Fiumicino (FCO) is the main hub, connected by the Leonardo Express train to Termini station.

visa_information.foreign_nationals: Italy is part of the Schengen Area. Check your local embassy for visa requirements based on your nationality.

weather.best_time_to_visit: Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) offer the most pleasant temperatures for walking.
weather.avoid: August, as it is extremely hot and many local businesses close for the summer holidays.
